content:
Formatting / presentation
* Please study the formatting syntax on this page: https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/statement , and format and present your statement properly. The Goal, Input, Output and Constraints sections all have to be updated.

Constraints
* Please either reduce the upper bound of [[N]] or introduce cases that sufficient cover the range between 5 and the upper bound. (It's not possible to have [[N]] = 500 anyway because of CG's character limit.)

* Please remove the time limit.

Stub generator
* Line 2: Please use `loopline` instead of `read`. See https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/stub-generator for the syntax.

Cases
* The expected output is currently either -1 or 0. Please introduce a variety of other cases where other output values are expected.

Cover picture
* Consider uploading a cover image for the puzzle.

Thanks.

Title
* The word "reactor" appears in the title and the Output section only. Consider changing "Reactor Core" to "Grid" in the title as the story isn't used.

Goal
* Please add a bullet point (•) before each operation for clarity.

* Please remove "Values can only increase" as it's implied by the operations and therefore redundant.

Output
* Please repalce "stabilize the reactor grid" with "equalize all values across the grid".

Constraints
* Please reduce 200 to 10. Again, it's not possible to have [[N]] as large as 200 (or even 100) due to the character limit. Also, [[N]] never exceeds 10 in the current cases.

* Please reduce 10^9 to 100, as the grid values never exceed 100 in the current cases.

Cases
* Please swap Pair 5 with Pair 1. The first pair of cases should be illustrative, so it's better to better to begin with a solvable grid instead of an impossible one.

* Please add cases with *larger grids* and *more randomised values*. One way to generate such cases is to work backwards:

  1. Start with a grid where all values are equal.
  2. Randomly select a row or column and decrease it by a random amount (≥1).
  3. Repeat until the grid appears sufficiently randomised.

Example for [[N]] = 5:
11 10 13 15 12
5 4 7 9 6
9 8 11 13 10
1 0 3 5 2
13 12 15 17 14

Example for [[N]] = 6:
27 6 22 36 21 28
21 0 16 30 15 22
25 4 20 34 19 26
37 16 32 46 31 38
22 1 17 31 16 23
34 13 29 43 28 35

* When adding the new cases, please make sure the expected outputs differ slightly between the test and validator cases within the same pair (e.g. ±5).

* Please add at least three pairs of cases:
  • Pair 1: [[N]] = 5 or 6, grid values between 0 and 50, expected output around 30

  • Pair 2: [[N]] = 7 or 8, grid values between 0 and 70, expected output around 60

  • Pair 3: [[N]] = 9 or 10, grid values between 0 and 100, expected output around 90

Cover picture
* Please update the cover image if the title is changed.

Thanks.

Updated list of outstanding matters:

Goal
* Please add a bullet point (•) before each operation for clarity.

Constraints
* Please reduce 100 to 10. Again, it's not possible to have [[N]] as large as 100 due to the character limit. Also, [[N]] never exceeds 10 in the current cases.

* Please reduce 10^9 to 100, as the grid values never exceed 100 in the current cases.

Cases
* Please remove Pair 6, as Test/Validator 5 are identical to Test/Validator 6. Then renumber the cases accordingly.

* Currently every test expects the same output as its corresponding validator. Please adjust the cases so that the expected outputs differ slightly within the same pair (e.g. ±5).

Thanks.
